_comment: Self-contained config for the harness's HEADLESS WORKTREE AGENTS, loaded by harness/run.ts runAgent() via `claude --settings <this file>` (command-line precedence = highest). It carries everything the autonomous agents need: (1) blanks ANTHROPIC_API_KEY so they authenticate on the Max-plan claude.ai login (the global ~/.claude env-block key is credit-starved); (2) the tool allowlist (adapted to this repo: panel/server/science/host live under python/synapse/; flywheel cycles also author scripts/, tests/, harness/notes/ specs+artifacts, harness/verify/ check verbs, and a harness/tasks.json task entry); (3) the never-push / never-merge / no-rm-rf / no-edit-VERSION denies PLUS a self-governance fence. These DENIES live HERE — not in .claude/settings.json — so the 'promotion is human' + 'no self-governance' safety binds the autonomous agents WITHOUT restricting the human's interactive session. The self-governance fence is deliberately PRECISE (not a blanket Edit(harness/**) deny, which would break a flywheel cycle's own refs — that deny was rejected in a prior session for exactly this reason): an agent may author cycle files, but may NOT edit its own permissions (agent-settings.json), the loop/governor (run.ts/daily.ts), VERSION, the green-ratchet floor (harness/verify/suite_baseline.json — else a sprint could lower its own bar), or runtime state under harness/state/** — which includes the human-only `ratified` flywheel authority (flywheel_queue.json), the completion ledger (done.json), and the Mode-B trigger (drop.json). Worktrees still inherit hooks from the committed .claude/settings.json.
